items.title: CMS previews tighter federal review for Medicaid 1115 waivers starting in 2027
items.summary: CMS issued new guidance saying it will require the CMS Chief Actuary to certify that Section 1115 demonstrations are budget neutral for any new waivers, renewals, or amendments approved on or after Jan. 1, 2027. CMS says it expects to start applying the approach described in the guidance while it works on a rule, giving states early notice for planning around 2027 renewals.

items.title: Medicaid work requirements rule: public comments still open
items.summary: CMS released an interim final rule on Medicaid work requirements with a public comment period; CMS summarizes the rule as covering a community engagement requirement for certain individuals. If you may be affected, watch for your state’s implementation timeline and submit comments before the federal deadline listed in the CMS fact sheet.

items.title: Social Security says it will finish moving benefit payments away from paper checks
items.summary: SSA says federal law and Executive Order 14247 require federal benefits to be paid electronically as of Sept. 30, 2025, and that Social Security plans to complete the transition for all beneficiaries this year. SSA cites Treasury data that printing and mailing a check costs about $3.07 per check and says paper checks are 16 times more likely to be lost, stolen, altered, or returned undeliverable than electronic payments.

items.title: Social Security Trustees: combined trust funds projected to pay full benefits until 2034
items.summary: SSA’s Trustees Report press release says the combined OASI and DI trust funds are projected to pay all scheduled benefits and administrative costs until 2034; at that point, 83% of scheduled benefits would be payable if Congress does not act. The release also says combined reserves declined by $160 billion in 2025 to $2.56 trillion, and the OASI (retirement) trust fund is projected to be depleted in Q4 2032 with 78% of benefits payable then.

items.title: HUD announces $4.04 billion homelessness assistance funding round (Continuum of Care)
items.summary: HUD announced a new Notice of Funding Opportunity for $4.04 billion for the Continuum of Care (CoC) homelessness assistance program. Local continuums typically apply through HUD’s CoC process, so communities should watch for the NOFO’s local timelines and required submissions.

items.title: HUD sets new EIV-SAVE attestation deadlines for housing providers
items.summary: HUD set deadlines for housing providers to review the EIV-SAVE Tenant Matching Report and attest documentation is in order: PBRA owners/agents must verify discrepancies by June 20, 2026, and Public Housing / Housing Choice Voucher agencies must complete the attestation by Aug. 3, 2026. NAHRO says the HCV/Public Housing attestation is completed through DocuSign and requires entries like the PHA code and submitter information.

items.title: IRS: 27 states opted into the new Federal Scholarship Tax Credit program
items.summary: The IRS says 27 states have elected to participate in the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit (FSTC) program, enacted under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill. The program allows eligible taxpayers to claim a federal tax credit of up to $1,700 for qualified contributions to Scholarship Granting Organizations (SGOs) that provide K-12 scholarships, but taxpayers must donate to an SGO in a participating state.

items.title: IRS Publication 505 highlights 2026 rule changes affecting premium tax credits and the Child Tax Credit
items.summary: IRS Publication 505 says that beginning in 2026, households with income over 400% of the federal poverty line are no longer eligible for the premium tax credit, and there is no longer a cap on how much excess advance credit must be repaid. The same publication says claiming the Child Tax Credit (CTC) or Additional CTC now requires a valid Social Security number (valid for employment and issued before the return due date, including extensions), with special rules for joint filers.

items.title: Education Department finalizes Workforce Pell for short-term job training programs
items.summary: The Education Department says Workforce Pell will allow Pell Grants for high-quality short-term programs starting July 1, 2026. A Department fact sheet says eligible workforce programs must be 8–14 weeks of instructional time and 150–599 clock hours, with early implementation allowed July 1, 2026 and the rule effective July 20, 2026.

items.title: USDA issues final rule updating WIC program operations and reporting
items.summary: USDA finalized a rule making a package of updates to WIC regulations, including streamlined federal reporting requirements for state agencies and clarified confidentiality rules to support coordination with public organizations and private physicians. USDA also says the rule incorporates legislation that gives state agencies the option to extend certification periods for breastfeeding women.
